title: Perplexity Just Built an AI That Does Everything
summary: Matthew Berman reviews Perplexity Computer, a new AI product claimed to handle multiple tasks. The video promotes the tool and links to Perplexity's product page for more details.
writeup: This video compares Perplexity Computer, a fully hosted AI agent platform, against self-hosted OpenClaw, arguing that the hosted approach is the right default for most practitioners. The presenter walks through Perplexity Computer's core architecture: threaded task contexts, hundreds of pre-built service connectors (Gmail, Google Calendar, Telegram, GitHub, Notion, and more), customizable skills, and a choice of frontier orchestrator models including Claude Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, and GPT-4.5. Four hands-on demos show the agent writing and executing code, scheduling a future research sweep, building a food-intake log from photos, and generating a benchmark comparison chart for newly released small models.
key_points: Perplexity Computer is a fully hosted AI agent environment that replicates the core capabilities of self-hosted OpenClaw (code writing and execution, web search, tool use, multi-thread context isolation) while eliminating the setup and credential-management burden that makes OpenClaw impractical for non-technical users.

The connectors system ships pre-built OAuth integrations with hundreds of services including Drive, Gmail, OneDrive, Box, Linear, Dropbox, Notion, GitHub, and Telegram, replacing the manual API-key plumbing required by OpenClaw and wrapping all service access inside Perplexity's own security layer.

Orchestrator model selection is user-configurable per thread, offering Claude Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, and GPT-4.5 as frontier options; the presenter runs Sonnet 4.6 as the primary orchestrator in both OpenClaw and Perplexity Computer, noting it delivers strong performance at lower cost than Opus.

Parallel sub-agent execution is built in: a single task can spawn multiple sub-agents, fan them out across web searches or code execution steps, then schedule a future wakeup (demonstrated with a UFC fight-night brief set to resume Saturday at 1:00 p.m. PST after a final research sweep).

Skills (reusable workflow definitions, analogous to system prompts for specific tasks) transfer directly from OpenClaw: the presenter installs a food-journal skill and an earnings-preview workflow into Perplexity Computer in seconds, both of which previously required manual setup on OpenClaw.

The food-journal demo shows the agent initializing a persistent markdown log from a photo of a meal, and the earnings-preview demo shows a Sunday 9:00 a.m. scheduled scan that fetches upcoming tech earnings, prompts for selection, pulls transcripts after earnings release, and delivers an analysis automatically.
